steps.title: Identify the parallel
steps.content: The speaker cites the kestrel “muting his mating-note to please the harmony of this sweet silence” in direct response to the friend describing his calm in the absence of strife. The key signal: nature mirrors the human experience of valuing peace.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: The kestrel demonstrates the natural world responding to the same peaceful silence that calms the friend, placing nature and human experience in alignment. The other choices either misread the passage's statement about shared understanding (A), claim unsupported things about nature's secrets (B), or wrongly characterize the passage's objective conditions as subjective ones (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
